sourceMovie.title: Under the Silver Lake
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Under the Silver Lake for the same reason it broke through in 2018: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a drama rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. David Robert Mitchell keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The crime and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Glass Onion: A Knives Out Mystery
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Under the Silver Lake clicked for you, Glass Onion: A Knives Out Mystery (2022) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and mystery, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Blue Velvet
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Under the Silver Lake clicked for you, Blue Velvet (1986) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and mystery, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Under the Silver Lake?
faq.answer: Try Glass Onion: A Knives Out Mystery, Blue Velvet, The Unforgivable—each shares DNA with Under the Silver Lake while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Under the Silver Lake?
faq.answer: Under the Silver Lake (2018) is best described as Crime, Drama, Mystery. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Under the Silver Lake worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
